ocr.text: In analyzing the outpatient visit volume trends and managed care enrollment data, it is crucial to highlight the evolving
landscape of healthcare services. The table illustrates significant changes in outpatient visit volumes, where the TotalVolume for
2021 was recorded at 1.2M visits, increasing to 1.4M visits by 2023. This growth suggests a robust demand for outpatient
services, which is further reflected in the 5-Year CAGR of 6.5% for this segment.

Additionally, the managed care enrollment data reveals shifts in patient demographics and coverage options, with
Enrollment(000s) capturing a notable increase. For instance, Enrollment(000s) is projected to reach 1250 in 2024, indicating a
growing reliance on managed care solutions. The implications of these trends extend to operational efficiencies, where the
OperatingMargin(%) for 2027 is projected at 19%, reflecting improved financial performance. Moreover, the RetentionRate(%)
for 2027 is expected to be 89%, suggesting that health plans are successfully maintaining their member base. As the industry
adapts to these changes, understanding the interplay between outpatient visit volumes and managed care enrollment becomes
essential for strategic planning and resource allocation.
